# desec_domain (Data Source)

Retrieves information about a specific deSEC DNS zone.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an existing domain by name
data "desec_domain" "example" {
  name = "example.com"
}

# Use the minimum TTL in other resources
output "minimum_ttl" {
  value = data.desec_domain.example.minimum_ttl
}

# Access DNSSEC key information
output "ds_records" {
  value = [for key in data.desec_domain.example.keys : key.ds if key.managed]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The domain name to look up.

### Read-Only

- `created` (String) Timestamp of domain creation in ISO 8601 format.
- `id` (String) The domain name, used as the data source identifier.
- `keys` (Attributes List) DNSSEC public key information for the domain. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--keys))
- `minimum_ttl` (Number) The minimum TTL (in seconds) that can be used for RRsets in this domain.
- `published` (String) Timestamp of when the domain's DNS records were last published.
- `touched` (String) Timestamp of when the domain's DNS records were last touched.

<a id="nestedatt--keys"></a>
### Nested Schema for `keys`

Read-Only:

- `dnskey` (String) The DNSKEY record content.
- `ds` (List of String) The DS record contents (for delegation).
- `managed` (Boolean) Whether this key is managed by deSEC.
